weather.tip: 94% rain chance tonight under overcast skies — carry an umbrella for the evening commute and give yourself extra time, since a heavy downpour can flood the usual low-lying junctions fast.

around.headline: Two men arrested for robbing BMTC bus passengers by faking vomiting fits
around.summary: Police say the duo would pretend to retch or vomit on a crowded bus, and while other passengers recoiled and looked away in disgust, an accomplice would pick pockets or slip bags away. Keep a hand on your bag reflexively if anyone near you suddenly causes a distracting scene on a bus.

around.headline: ED conducts searches in Bengaluru linked to IS recruitment and terror-funding case
around.summary: The Enforcement Directorate carried out raids in the city today as part of a probe into alleged terror recruitment and funding. No public safety alert has been issued, but expect more details as the investigation proceeds.

around.headline: Minor fire breaks out at Nagasandra Metro Station
around.summary: A small fire was reported at the Nagasandra Metro Station on the Green Line today. If you use this station, keep an eye out for any residual service adjustments over the next day or two.

around.headline: 18-storey Harlur apartment tower to be demolished after developing a tilt
around.summary: A ready-to-move-in tower in Harlur will be razed just three months after receiving its occupancy certificate, after residents noticed it tilting. If you're flat-hunting, it's worth checking a building's structural sign-off history, not just its OC.
around.source: The Hindu / The Times of India

around.headline: Karnataka restricts pigeon feeding to notified spots only
around.summary: New state guidelines say pigeons can now only be fed at designated locations, citing health risks from droppings and feathers. Feeding them elsewhere in public spaces could now draw civic action.

around.headline: US imposes fresh 10% tariff on Indian goods; Sensex tumbles 900 points
around.summary: Markets fell sharply today — Nifty50 closed below 23,800 — after Washington announced a new 10% tariff on Indian imports, adding to existing trade tensions. If you hold equity mutual funds or a SIP, expect near-term volatility rather than reacting to a single day's move.

events.name: India International Travel Mart — Bengaluru Edition
events.note: Last chance to catch this travel and tourism expo with multiple state pavilions if you're comparing holiday packages.

events.name: Bengaluru's first city-wide busking programme
events.note: Street musicians are now being formally hosted at spots around the city — worth a stroll through the usual public squares and parks to catch a set.

events.name: IITM Bengaluru 2026
events.note: A public exposition at Palace Grounds if you're looking for a weekend outing.

events.name: Extra Onam special trains via Bengaluru
events.note: The Bengaluru–Ernakulam Vande Bharat is getting 16 coaches and Railways is adding more special trains for the Onam rush — book early if you're headed to Kerala.

street_smart.title: You're standing in a crowded BMTC bus. A passenger near you suddenly starts retching like they're about to be sick, and everyone — including you — instinctively recoils and looks away. What do you do?
street_smart.based_on: Real case reported today: Bengaluru police arrested two men for robbing BMTC bus passengers using exactly this trick — one fakes vomiting to create disgust and distraction while an accomplice picks pockets or grabs bags (Public TV English).
street_smart.situation: The bus is packed, people are shuffling back to avoid the mess, and in the crush of bodies you can't tell who's brushing past you on purpose.

choices.text: Keep a hand on your bag or pocket as you step back, stay alert to anyone crowding in close during the commotion, and check your belongings once things settle.
choices.feedback: Correct. The fake-sickness act works specifically because the disgust reflex makes people let go of situational awareness — the moment you flinch away is the moment an accomplice moves in. Keeping physical contact with your bag through the distraction defeats the trick's entire mechanism.

choices.text: Turn away completely and push toward the door to get clear of the mess, leaving your bag loose at your side.
choices.feedback: This is exactly what the scam counts on. Turning your back and dropping your guard on your bag is the split-second window the accomplice needs — the vomiting is staged specifically to make you do this.

choices.text: Loudly accuse the sick passenger of faking it in front of everyone, without checking your own belongings first.
choices.feedback: You might be right, but a public confrontation still leaves your bag unattended in your hand or at your feet during the shouting — check your own belongings are secure before doing anything else.

choices.text: Move to help the 'sick' passenger directly, putting a hand on their shoulder or back.
choices.feedback: Leaning in to help hands over exactly the closeness and distraction the pair are engineering, and frees both your hands from your belongings. A simple step back with a hand on your bag protects you just as well without the risk.

takeaways: Any sudden disgust or distraction in a crowd — vomiting, fainting, a spilled drink — is a classic pickpocket setup; keep a hand on your bag on reflex.
Wear bags crossbody with the zip facing your body on crowded buses and metros, not slung loosely over one shoulder.
Report anything suspicious to the conductor immediately and file a complaint with BMTC or the nearest police station if something goes missing.

quiz.q: What trick did two men arrested today use to distract BMTC bus passengers before robbing them?
options: Faking a phone call for help
quiz.why: The disgust reflex made nearby passengers turn away and lower their guard, giving an accomplice the opening to pick pockets or grab bags.

quiz.q: How many storeys is the Harlur apartment tower being demolished after it developed a tilt?
quiz.why: The tower is being razed only about three months after it received its occupancy certificate.

quiz.q: What new tariff did the US impose on Indian goods today, contributing to a market sell-off?
quiz.why: The Nifty50 closed below 23,800 the same day, with the Sensex down around 900 points.

quiz.q: Unless dissolved earlier, what is the term length of India's Lok Sabha?
quiz.why: The Rajya Sabha, by contrast, is a permanent body whose members serve staggered 6-year terms.

quiz.q: Roughly what percentage of Earth's surface is covered by water?
quiz.why: About 97% of that water is saline, held in the oceans, with only a small fraction as fresh water.
